successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave-api/leave.dto.ts exists and exports CreateLeaveRequestDto, ApproveLeaveRequestDto, RejectLeaveRequestDto, CancelLeaveRequestDto, DiscardLeaveRequestDto, LeaveRequestResponse, LeaveBalanceResponse with class-validator decorators on DTOs.

src/modules/leave-api/leave.controller.ts exists and exports LeaveController class with methods submitLeave, approveLeave, rejectLeave, cancelLeave, discardDraft, getBalances, getHistory, each delegating to ILeaveManagementService and handling errors.

src/modules/leave-api/leave.routes.ts exists and exports registerLeaveRoutes function that registers all seven REST endpoints with appropriate HTTP methods, paths, and auth middleware (authenticate + authorize with correct roles).
tests/unit/modules/leave-api/leave.controller.test.ts exists and contains Jest tests covering success and error scenarios for all controller methods, using mocked ILeaveManagementService.
All endpoints validate request bodies using class-validator and return 400 on validation failure.
RBAC is enforced: employee-only endpoints (submit, cancel, discard, history, balances) require 'employee' role; manager-only endpoints (approve, reject) require 'manager' role.
All state-changing operations (submit, approve, reject, cancel, discard) are delegated to ILeaveManagementService, which is responsible for audit logging (GP-002) and transaction management.
No unhandled promise rejections; all async errors are caught and return 500 with a generic error message (no sensitive data leaked).

constraints: All state-changing operations must be delegated to ILeaveManagementService, which is responsible for audit logging (GP-002) and transaction management.
Input validation using class-validator must be applied in each controller method before calling the service, returning 400 on validation errors (GP-003).
RBAC must be enforced: employee-only endpoints require 'employee' role; manager-only endpoints require 'manager' role (GP-005).
All async errors must be caught and handled, returning appropriate HTTP error responses without unhandled rejections (GP-006).
No sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII, financial data) must be logged (GP-004).
outOfScope: Implementing the ILeaveManagementService methods (submitLeaveRequest, approveLeaveRequest, etc.)
Database access, repository implementations, or any infrastructure code
Phase 3-6: Web client scaffold, employee view, manager view, or any frontend code
Any UI components or browser-side logic
